## House Still in Play, Leans GOP
Control of the U.S. House of Representatives has [not yet been determined](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOwyAQBNCvgY5oF4hNCopr3OXaKy0C67AnDJFNEuXvT0l_3YxGesX8nOdz4ML1-h1W8n1pswZtFaJCmFPg8pqtTH4h0mQkeRwNauPM0cns3WU8WYoXBzZCCoQEQEMyBm0agUiyf2uICIhGG3uwwdFxCUM82UDuEoQFStzbdvhtXHumpbzag7ZDbKssPvd-24X5EnoSeuqZMpfy3oSeclup0nP_xPtOQk_25GAYQH26iq32rRW10Rq47upeE0VOlISe5ObT7cml8D6MwsJ1DfyBZf_vhIfXfwEAAP__vNRe-Q), as several critical races remain too close to call. As of last night, Republicans held 216 House seats to Democrats' 208, with 11 undecided. The GOP leads in 8 of those, while Democrats lead in 3. Most of those still uncertain are [in California](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFugzAQQL_GbEac7RAyeOjClq4d0cWc4aIDI-yG8vdVund-0tPT-7oPd2ThdfrEhXyJaTCNcRpAQzOMyHIOrhp9JDJkK_JwtWBsZy9dNftbsObqIsTWIcbGBmwvDwohtAg3GruK_dsGAA2ANdbVDju6RGzDzSF1D1SuoZFL2utn4rXMFOVML9rrkJZK_FzKlpX9UKZXpj-Oo47pZ6Ujv7ky_ZaEC4esTI8imk7KOqBwTPvKqOf0nUkv-Ew7l1PnwiJ65nWirAtPc9E7BsrV7sftYBHO7VW5ZlqQ5a-g_Hfk5c1vAAAA__8l_2aw), including districts around the Los Angeles metropolitan area. Democrats are hoping for a razor-thin majority, but Republican officials are confident that the GOP will retain its majority. A final count will almost certainly have to wait until [next week, at the earliest,](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2O6yAUhJ_G7oj4OWC7oLhNutx2S-sYDjYrbCwgifL2K2-_5cxIo0_f12N-YEzxWP_jTraFPEsugQnBBJ89xvSZofc2EElSPVkxKCHVqPTYb1aRR-2D0UapgMGNxnA9LQv3RhlQoY_2ehNCcCGUVHADHEkHNG4CpHHBDjj52HK5fed4tI1C-uQXlZvLe5_s1tpZO_Wvk_dO3vE86F2v6QqlRZeok_ctPysxl49WcmKtPPeTUSLXYj7q1a-FamWVDmzEDLkJDAL3chkmgMmA1mTcJAMOIui-WH--Y0qxmqEDvu4Y0y9O-0vPy8qfAAAA__8i_WdN) when Congress is back in session and prepares to elect its new leaders.
## Biden and Trump to Meet
President**** Joe Biden and President-elect Donald Trump [will meet Wednesday](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0j7FurDAQRb_G7oyYsTGmcPGa7fa1KdEsHhZHNl6BA-Lvo43Spr1XOkfn4z7eKaa4Pv9TZl_nMmKLRgEoaMdAMV2jkcHPzMhasodeA2qnOycXD6xhmB_TQBqsm4I1bPu-w6lHGAwMMvo3DQBaAI3aNIYcdzPZaTDE7kHCtBxiLVvzWeJaF57TVQ7emqlkmfxS62sX-p_Am8DbyufeXLSU8r5_B4G3Rwy8qrp95ZfKzFWdHFbeA12qHJQU2E6js2CapeYkNx9eZ0wp7rYXpn1miunHV__qPzx-BwAA__9FNVxR) in the Oval Office, the White House announced on Saturday. In [a speech on Thursday](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOqzAQRb_GdEaMxwancPGkJ7qsttsSGTyEWTk4sp1E_P2K7be6xZWOjs7Xdbp6jrzfPvydXF3TpDqlJYCEbgqe4zHpJriVSBE25GBAUGjR2GZziN53AfoFabmgNQP0YIMetLWgZ4MNu5MGAB0AKtSt9pbM6vvloj3Z2QvdUeCacvudeK8brfFIL8rtku5NdFutjyLwn1CjUKOfl53epb2l8xZq_EyRKy9FqPGRqXCgvcr5HPneuJLc0rOQ9CFkKkXW7ZlL8IdQY6kpHwJHDgL_AxhzMTCYJrvweHOMXPpB6O529xx_VepfaV5O_QQAAP__sy1llA), Biden said he assured Trump “ _that I would direct my entire administration to work with his team to ensure a peaceful and orderly transition_.” The traditional post-election meeting of the incoming and outgoing presidents will take place at 11:00 ET. Trump will become the second president to serve two non-consecutive terms when he becomes the nation's 47th president. The other was Grover Cleveland, the 22nd and 24th.
## Home Prices, State by State
A new analysis shows median home prices in the U.S. ranging from $167,110 in West Virginia to $851,930 in Hawaii. The median**** price in August for a single-family house was $385,000. A University of Hawaii [report](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00L-OszAQBPCnMZ0jvDZ_Urj4Grp87ZVog5ewd4ZFtpMcb3_i-mumGemn0Xzcxhty5O3xH1fyZZYRanDaGG3qMSDHY3RV8DMRkK3Im84asL1t-mrxLriuuRO1gK4PJlzxalpqgQCAWtdV7E_NGFMbY8G6i8Oemhnb6eqQ-jsqV1PgIunyKbyVheZ4yIvSZZK1in4pZc_K_lMwKBhmSeW50dkpGE5ZwVDbMxoFw3s5NGe94BuZ9SLPzNtDr5i-qOgsmr532jK_SO9JZspZkqZJNll5ygqGKvmwvzlGzm2nXP1YkePvkPLXMS8PPwEAAP__vEllRw) shines some light on The Aloha State's sky-high expenses; regulatory costs, including lengthy permitting processes and strict zoning laws, account for more than half the price of a new condominium. California came in second, with a median home price of $776,000 and some of the least affordable metropolitan areas, including Los Angeles and San Jose. Every state is shown [on this map](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LFugzAQxvGngc3IZzuEDB66ZEvXjuiwD7jKYIQvoLx9lezdP_306f_z6B_IidfpGxfyMubeaOMUgALdR-T06l0d_UhkyNbk4WrB2M5eunr2OproAkR3heDiAG5oW8TgOoBO30Zds39rAKABrLGucdjRZcQ23BxSN2DlNEWWvDe_mVeZaUyvfNDehLzUyc8iW6nsV2Xulbmf59kcXJ6YAm4smLjIe1iZ-4LbRlEtFBlXNeeFVMFEats5kBpe6qmKKoJCH8pOOU-J-oOnlUSo3n3cTk6JS3utnJ4W5PT5IP81Obz5CwAA__8JamW2), published by Visual Capitalist.

clean_body: Control of the U.S. House of Representatives has not yet been determined, as several critical races remain too close to call. | | | | | | SUNDAY, November 10, 2024 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1975, the freighter S.S. Edmund Fitzgerald sank in Lake Superior during a massive storm, killing all 29 of the crew. The singer Gordon Lightfoot memorialized the tragedy in Speaking of light feet, today's video shows mascots running full speed while playing football against some fully-padded kids. We hope you as much as we did! We're sponsored this weekend by Check out their free wealth protection guide in today's edition! | House Still in Play, Leans GOP Control of the U.S. House of Representatives has , as several critical races remain too close to call. As of last night, Republicans held 216 House seats to Democrats' 208, with 11 undecided. The GOP leads in 8 of those, while Democrats lead in 3. Most of those still uncertain are , including districts around the Los Angeles metropolitan area. Democrats are hoping for a razor-thin majority, but Republican officials are confident that the GOP will retain its majority. A final count will almost certainly have to wait until when Congress is back in session and prepares to elect its new leaders. Biden and Trump to Meet President** Joe Biden and President-elect Donald Trump in the Oval Office, the White House announced on Saturday. In , Biden said he assured Trump “ that I would direct my entire administration to work with his team to ensure a peaceful and orderly transition.” The traditional post-election meeting of the incoming and outgoing presidents will take place at 11:00 ET. Trump will become the second president to serve two non-consecutive terms when he becomes the nation's 47th president. The other was Grover Cleveland, the 22nd and 24th. Home Prices, State by State A new analysis shows median home prices in the U.S. ranging from $167,110 in West Virginia to $851,930 in Hawaii. The median price in August for a single-family house was $385,000. A University of Hawaii shines some light on The Aloha State's sky-high expenses; regulatory costs, including lengthy permitting processes and strict zoning laws, account for more than half the price of a new condominium. California came in second, with a median home price of $776,000 and some of the least affordable metropolitan areas, including Los Angeles and San Jose. clean_body: READ >> | | | Without Elon, our country might be in a VERY different situation right now. We owe him a debt of gratitude for his fearless work for OUR NEXT PRESIDENT! But he’s noticed a CRITICAL strategic advantage in local elections... Something that very few others have. And Soros is planning to EXPLOIT this advantage to a level that we have NEVER seen before. You see, Musk called Soros out BIG TIME! “[Soros]wants nothing less than the destruction of western civilization.” “Parents don’t understand the soviet level of indoctrination their children are receiving.” “[Soros realized] the value for money in local races is much higher than it is in national races.” We are going to go toe-to-toe with Soros and defeating his war chest by pooling support from Grassroots Conservatives like you. We’ve already beaten Soros and his billionaire radical leftist friends over 250 times and liberated more than 4.5 MILLION students from radical leftist leadership in their schools. And that’s what we’re going to do here at the 1776 Project PAC. We could use your help as we are already gearing up for our next round of elections around 100 days from NOW! -1776 Alerts | | Paid for by 1776 Project PAC PO Box 2891 Springfield, VA 22152Not Authorized by any candidate or campaign committee Republican Majority PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

name: OKGOP
subject: Happy 249th Birthday U.S. Marines
body:
| [ ](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=urG6o0YzHACIJY0G92IOaIOiUFGhm1XQHaiZ59fvCoY&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_249th_birthday_u_s_mar&n=1)
---
Peter --
Today, we pause to honor the proud legacy of the United States Marine Corps. For 249 years, the Marine Corps has held a unique and vital role in defending our nation, shaped by a rich history, unwavering commitment, and the enduring spirit of the men and women who serve. This day is more than just a birthday; it is a tribute to generations of Marines who have bravely answered the call to protect freedom at home and abroad. The Marine Corps was born on November 10, 1775, at Tun Tavern in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. The Continental Congress, recognizing the need for a force that could fight on both land and sea, established the Marines to serve as an elite fighting force within the Navy. From these humble beginnings, the Marine Corps has evolved into one of the world’s most respected military branches, known for its discipline, honor, and adaptability. One of the Corps' defining moments came during World War I at the Battle of Belleau Wood. With their fierce fighting spirit and resilience under extreme pressure, the Marines earned the nickname "Devil Dogs" from German forces—a name that would forever symbolize their determination and courage. This battle set the tone for the Marines' future, establishing their reputation as a force that, no matter the odds, would stand firm and prevail. In World War II, the Marines solidified their legacy, taking on some of the most challenging and brutal battles in modern warfare. The Marines showcased not only their fighting skills but also their ability to adapt to the harshest conditions. The iconic image of Marines raising the flag on Mount Suribachi at Iwo Jima is a lasting symbol of American resilience and resolve, inspiring future generations to serve with pride. Today’s Marines continue to build on this legacy, adapting to the modern battlefield with the same honor, courage, and commitment that have defined the Corps for centuries. They are often the first to respond in times of crisis, whether it's a humanitarian mission, disaster relief, or direct combat. Their presence around the world stands as a testament to the United States’ commitment to protecting peace, ensuring security, and aiding those in need. The Marine Corps Birthday is a moment for us all to reflect on the sacrifices made by Marines, past and present. It’s an opportunity to honor not only their service but also the values they embody: loyalty, duty, and an unwavering commitment to the greater good. Each year, Marines come together to celebrate this legacy with the traditional reading of Major General John A. Lejeune’s birthday message, reminding them of the history they carry forward and the traditions they uphold. As we celebrate this day, let us also remember the families who stand behind every Marine, providing strength, love, and resilience. Their sacrifices are inseparable from those of the Marines they support. Together, they form a community bonded by shared values, duty, and an unbreakable commitment to our country. Thank you to all Marines and their families. Your legacy is a profound part of our nation’s history, and we are forever grateful for your service. "Oorah" and Happy 249th Birthday Marines! From your friends at the OKGOP,
God Bless

This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com). To stop receiving emails, [click here](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/unsubscribe?e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_249th_birthday_u_s_mar&n=2).
You can also keep up with OKGOP on [Twitter](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=JcrYNQ7dhdufXPgOgJxNGHSsRONv6mnq7ly6aBOUvGVprSj7jfHNOpP70kR_1QLA&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_249th_birthday_u_s_mar&n=3) or [Facebook](https://okgop.nationbuilder.com/r?u=UQNagEd-DqbIXa37T8ODid9mG9NGbq3z9te4_riKc6EaGmrOrRzKc2wAaVTFbzRY&e=48f9f7a040435275f8f9fa993b0a5712&utm_source=okgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=2_happy_249th_birthday_u_s_mar&n=4).

name: History Facts
subject: Did Vikings really wear horned helmets?
body:
When you think about Vikings, you may picture horned helmets — and vice versa. [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68beyeyo5sjtrhcmlc90n3e7fi7bte7cg2p7s5ztlcvbqdnpk3a3gzljred6nizc2ta8fgdrj9ztne7efv0mofo85pxjgtp41ahra7bfkoppis6jcm7fh3thoqpt4d3ojj9op0zpj9gx4tzjo&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [Vikings didn’t actually wear horned helmets.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [World History](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| W hen you think about Vikings, you may picture horned helmets — and vice versa. The horns are a staple of Viking imagery in comics, sports mascots, and Halloween costumes, but there’s actually no evidence that they were widely used.
---
Horned helmets first entered the Viking mythos in 1876, when artist Carl Emil Doepler [included](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) them in his iconic costume designs for German composer Richard Wagner’s _Der Ring des Nibelungen_ , also known as the Ring Cycle, an epic tale of Norse mythology told over the course of four different operas. (Even if you’re not familiar with the work, you’ve probably heard “Ride of the Valkyries,” which opens up the last act of the second opera, _Die Walküre,_ or _The Valkyries_.) Wagner’s opera was so wildly influential on popular culture that the helmets became associated with Vikings in the popular consciousness.
Then, in 1942, two workers were harvesting peat from a bog near Veksø, Denmark, when they [uncovered](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) a pair of horned helmets, which were originally attributed to Vikings, supposedly adding a little scientific backup to the popular lore. Alas, 80 years later, new analysis showed that the helmets dated back to around 900 BCE, some 1,700 years before the first Vikings. Without those artifacts, there isn’t any strong archaeological evidence that indicates Vikings ever wore horned helmets.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## Not all Vikings were Scandinavian.
Being a Viking was a way of life (the word itself comes from the Old Norse _víkingr_ , meaning “pirate” or “raider”), not a particular race, and not all Vikings hailed from modern-day Scandinavia (Norway, Sweden, and Denmark). A [six-year](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=smqhks&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) study of the remains of 442 people who lived in Europe and Greenland during the Viking Age (roughly 800 to 1050 CE) found that while inland communities tended to be of Scandinavian descent, coastal Vikings had genetic heritage from Asia and southern Europe, too. The Vikings weren’t one single civilization, either: The same study found that smaller groups tended to stick together.

The deep state, the fake news media, and the rest of the radical left have been put on notice.

Our country is going to be made great again, **and it 's going to be made great again soon.**

But for now, we need to stand together and say thank you to the man who made it all possible- **President Donald J. name: Hillsdale College via Old Glory Today
subject: The greatest armed conflict in human history
body:
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us.

This sponsored message from Old Glory Today is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/c8-8k-byFDqtBOaKh0LvUA~~/AARclAA~/RgRpE1UGP0TEaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmVkLmhpbGxzZGFsZS5lZHUvaGlsbHNkYWxlL3N1cHBvcnQtdGhlLXNlY29uZC13b3JsZC13YXJzP3NjPTAwNDY5TjAyNDJMMTVET0RFRVMmdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1wcm9zcGVjdGluZyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1zZWNvbmR3b3JsZHdhcnNfZHZkJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PVBTX1BBLU9HVC0yMDI0MTExMFcDc3BjQgpnK-S5MGfUHngoUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~) Dear Fellow American,
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This fact means that we’re losing more and more living memory of the greatest armed conflict in human history, which makes the study of World War II even more important.
To this end, Hillsdale College has produced an online course called **“The Second World Wars.”**
**Taught by historian Victor Davis Hanson** , this course explores how the conflict’s diverse theaters, belligerents, and ways of fighting came eventually to define a single war. And it examines the key investments, strategies, and leaders that led the Allied powers to victory over the Axis. [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/c8-8k-byFDqtBOaKh0LvUA~~/AARclAA~/RgRpE1UGP0TEaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmVkLmhpbGxzZGFsZS5lZHUvaGlsbHNkYWxlL3N1cHBvcnQtdGhlLXNlY29uZC13b3JsZC13YXJzP3NjPTAwNDY5TjAyNDJMMTVET0RFRVMmdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1wcm9zcGVjdGluZyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1zZWNvbmR3b3JsZHdhcnNfZHZkJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PVBTX1BBLU9HVC0yMDI0MTExMFcDc3BjQgpnK-S5MGfUHngoUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~) If you’d like to secure a DVD set before they’re all gone, you can get one shipped directly to your home for any gift of $100 or more to Hillsdale College.
Here’s a secure link to reserve your copy of **“The Second World Wars”** on DVD:
**[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/support-the-second-world-wars](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/c8-8k-byFDqtBOaKh0LvUA~~/AARclAA~/RgRpE1UGP0TEaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmVkLmhpbGxzZGFsZS5lZHUvaGlsbHNkYWxlL3N1cHBvcnQtdGhlLXNlY29uZC13b3JsZC13YXJzP3NjPTAwNDY5TjAyNDJMMTVET0RFRVMmdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1wcm9zcGVjdGluZyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1zZWNvbmR3b3JsZHdhcnNfZHZkJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PVBTX1BBLU9HVC0yMDI0MTExMFcDc3BjQgpnK-S5MGfUHngoUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)**
Thank you for preserving liberty for future generations of Americans through education with Hillsdale College!
Best regards,
Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D. Executive Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College
P.S. The **“The Second World Wars”** DVD set is great as a homeschool curriculum or for independent study. Plus, it makes a perfect gift for anyone who wants to know more about the Second World War. **[Reserve your copy now.](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/c8-8k-byFDqtBOaKh0LvUA~~/AARclAA~/RgRpE1UGP0TEaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmVkLmhpbGxzZGFsZS5lZHUvaGlsbHNkYWxlL3N1cHBvcnQtdGhlLXNlY29uZC13b3JsZC13YXJzP3NjPTAwNDY5TjAyNDJMMTVET0RFRVMmdXRtX3NvdXJjZT1wcm9zcGVjdGluZyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1zZWNvbmR3b3JsZHdhcnNfZHZkJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PVBTX1BBLU9HVC0yMDI0MTExMFcDc3BjQgpnK-S5MGfUHngoUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)**

USO Supporter,
On November 10, 1775, at the Tun Tavern in Philadelphia, the U. S. Marine Corps was born. And on Sunday, we honor "The Few and The Proud" by celebrating the Marine Corps' 249th birthday.
**[Will you sign the Marine Corps' birthday card now to show your gratitude for our brave Marines through the years?](https://links.e.uso.)**[ ](https://links.e.uso.)
[SIGN THE MARINE CORPS BIRTHDAY CARD](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/A-VKBb0LyvfL/MDNwQmdHS203aVMzTzRQZkNDU0dYU3NrSDNjUUlUdU1zYU4yRmNWSERqb0tjYXdtWjMwNzNNWkZEazJVZmJkRmwzZXpIdHdJSEtMRHVSZ0RqLzFsWWdLeDhRVHRYRmEyTXdFQ0VIQjBNQTQ9S0/aTRSUEJJTGd4RGNCb3N1djdSZHBIYWx4VnBvTW4ranM5N1o0M3Y2UzRlZG1LaGhXRVY3aURHL0Z0Tkh2T25jYUpZQVpSdEtWSXgzajI2a04xYkFTbi9Ib1FjdmN5YWVDS0)
---
The Marine Corps' birthday is a big deal here at the USO, not only to celebrate the storied history of the Marines but also to honor the sacrifice of thousands of active Marines who stand at the ready and are currently deployed, often far from their loved ones.
**[That's why we set a goal to reach 50,000 signatures via email and text message through Sunday. Please, will you sign the card to wish the USMC a happy 249th birthday and honor service members around the globe? ](https://links.e.uso.)**
[SIGN THE MARINE CORPS BIRTHDAY CARD](https://links.e.uso.org/els/v2/A-VKBb0LyvfL/MDNwQmdHS203aVMzTzRQZkNDU0dYU3NrSDNjUUlUdU1zYU4yRmNWSERqb0tjYXdtWjMwNzNNWkZEazJVZmJkRmwzZXpIdHdJSEtMRHVSZ0RqLzFsWWdLeDhRVHRYRmEyTXdFQ0VIQjBNQTQ9S0/aTRSUEJJTGd4RGNCb3N1djdSZHBIYWx4VnBvTW4ranM5N1o0M3Y2UzRlZG1LaGhXRVY3aURHL0Z0Tkh2T25jYUpZQVpSdEtWSXgzajI2a04xYkFTbi9Ib1FjdmN5YWVDS0)
---
Thank you for supporting our military, and Semper Fi!
--The USO

name: The Editors at Broad + Liberty
subject: The biggest winner of last week's election
body:
News + editorial to start your week | Broad + Liberty Weekly Reads | | | |
---
| | | Good morning and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Weekly Reads.

[Subscribe to our daily email list](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4cb6e16500&e=b7b3eddce2) and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
---
|
### **1.[Dave McCormick won. Bob Casey should not drag the state through a pointless recount](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=059b82d831&e=b7b3eddce2)**
_By Kyle Sammin_
The race between Donald Trump and Kamala Harris in Pennsylvania was tight. The race between Dave McCormick and Bob Casey was even tighter. But at the end of the day, both Republicans were leading by an amount unlikely to be overturned. By this afternoon, that “unlikely” is becoming “impossible.” Casey’s people say their man has[ a path to victory](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=31e32acecd&e=b7b3eddce2) if the few uncounted votes left are in their favor. They’re wrong, and should concede now and save the taxpayers the expense of a recount. **Why It Matters. **But Pennsylvania’s election system, while still[ deeply flawed](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=977f41720a&e=b7b3eddce2), has improved since 2020. They started counting the mail vote at the beginning of the day, not after polls closed. That means they are already a day ahead of the 2020 pace. Moreover, with the pandemic ended, fewer people voted by mail, so there are fewer to count in the first place. The rules are more certain, thanks to state supreme court rulings, with less wiggle room to challenge the exclusion of improper ballots. An added thorn in Casey’s side is that this time, unlike 2020, Trump told his voters to vote early. The mail vote is still more Democratic than Republican, but not by nearly as much. There is one final wrinkle that will seal the deal for McCormick. In Cambria County (which Trump carried by 37 percentage points in 2020)[ mechanical difficulties](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ff4e92f599&e=b7b3eddce2) mean that the whole county is still being hand-counted. Two-thirds of that deeply Republican county — around 40 or 50 thousand ballots — is still yet to be reported. Experts say[ Cambria will net McCormick 20,000 votes](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e61f022d46&e=b7b3eddce2). It’s over — for Kamala and for Bob.

[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=8b51eac8eb&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
### **2.[The biggest winner of this week’s election is the alternative media](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=5cfcd703e7&e=b7b3eddce2)**

_By Beth Ann Rosica_
There were big winners and losers this week, but perhaps the most important victory goes to the alternative media outlets who do not succumb to being the mouthpiece for a particular political party. Even the mainstream media outlets are acknowledging they have a problem. Brian Stetler of[ CNN](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=7697175f65&e=b7b3eddce2) wrote the morning after the election, “[Trump’s] defeat of Kamala Harris is raising questions about the media’s credibility, influence, and audience. Some of the questions might not be answerable for years.” Jeff Bezos, the owner of _The Washington Post_ , penned an[ op-ed](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e61cca547b&e=b7b3eddce2) with a similar message. “In the annual public surveys about trust and reputation, journalists and the media have regularly fallen near the very bottom, often just above Congress. But in this year’s Gallup poll, we have managed to fall below Congress. Our profession is now the least trusted of all. Something we are doing is clearly not working.” **Why It Matters.** Locally, I like to think that _Broad + Liberty_ plays a similar role — of course, I am biased since I write here every week. _[Broad + Liberty](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=0711f2ad2b&e=b7b3eddce2)_ was founded on the principles to “promote innovative ideas, diverse and disruptive viewpoints, and positive policies that hit home for our readers, supporters and followers.” While many of our editorial pieces are right-leaning, we are always willing and typically eager to publish both sides. Occasionally, we run point and counterpoint stories to illustrate different perspectives. And our investigative journalism is consistently focused on presenting the facts and uncovering information that other news outlets are often unwilling to write about. We diligently differentiate between fact and opinion.

[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=d296e5ee8b&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* **[Christine Flowers: My new best friends](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ab2b904006&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Guy Ciarrocchi: McCormick is the leader Pennsylvania needs](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=4f38f9bfdf&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[David P. Hardy: Philadelphia teachers — like their students — want educational choice](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=d49b20d450&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Jeff Hurvitz: Philadelphia’s bike path to obstruction](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=76e1f34b42&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Thom Nickels: Philadelphia’s grande dame of the essay](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ec93b84079&e=b7b3eddce2)**

---
|
---
|
# **4\. In the News**
_Broad + Liberty_ Chief Investigative Reporter Todd Shepherd was [quoted in _Columbia Journalism Review_](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=131f4cfc01&e=b7b3eddce2), "I find it obscene that an outlet like [Courier] asks their readers for $10 and $20 contributions when they’re running million-dollar Facebook campaigns like this, like they’re asking their readers to support their ‘journalism.’”

[Continue reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e3c4be6436&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
# **5\. What we're reading**
Where to begin, right? Like most of you, we’re reading our share of post-election analysis. Against all odds, Donald Trump is returning to the White House and everyone wants to tell us why. Dan McLaughlin at _National Review_ writes this week that [Democrats made a lot of bad choices that drove voters away](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=bcf3ec3742&e=b7b3eddce2). Josh Barro writes a similar take at his Substack, arguing that Democrats “[deserved to lose this election](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=904ad1a9fc&e=b7b3eddce2), even if Trump did not deserve to win it.” At _The Free Press_ , River Page breaks down [Trump’s strategy of reaching young voters through alternative media](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=516227b16d&e=b7b3eddce2). Harris’s decision not to go on Joe Rogan’s show looks as consequential as Hillary not campaigning in Wisconsin. There’s so much more, and more to come in the coming weeks — including right here at _Broad + Liberty_. Stay tuned!

|
---

body:

---
| | | [ ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW9WL4ySifRyEQyG7D6ZDcaKmFQcO_SqeUFRkYFn0WrvHHisWiwN-mSj2nwLaaiVAndhLwjC3x9MI2KUAa0DnJViqSOAPtdfRJrzMKtRCwblAW9CQ2sdZ9no1vJGozE0qKEsEuDLqp-xiTdTVPEWnhd1Ew90dhpJMUKc1xYkfQGDL3_UZn9Cj_QCyH9MOCdkWrt_c2gMeThKgQehbFehu6YNCdgnBy-XwwPDOPXJfCFEWCxUSDypTnJ1m198AjSiJQCtmceZWDN9kVYtX6pTLZHHE1bLV6BKkaajMCbRL2BW29NG1fMPJ_5MBUEhg6SrdWzdTdaxcXT_i27UXWyqQFvPjdxmSZSIK4YZYAqaPXMXMIDUuperp-Snua5ogH4DN0relOeA5T3G4k-X91El8RkTb0Vr_uDBWe8SE2u10Su1sV3As-z4runv4bFrW34VatGFsFo3-Lo-A9k8p54ws8h4UlBm6YWZxrSCBcFV2VACK/4bc/_aAK8RJgTj6bVomq7yW3_g/h0/h001.cUZz2YAgIVGu5aeXrbGYqouRFI4L5q4-akqTg31PBuQ)
---
1️⃣ **_Pennsylvania:_ Democrats protect Pennsylvania House Majority **
In one of the most competitive state legislative chambers in the country, the DLCC and Pennsylvania House Democrats have successfully secured enough seats to protect their crucial majority and keep this chamber under Democratic control. [Republicans failed to gain a single seat after spending millions to flip this critical chamber → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW62rQa9Pz0Y7yher5LoycxnbL5dndfalsAVRkTHdyI2D4WHasA4G-N_xWKikj44FfzYpbET_sHPWbLaPQ1xf7jetABz3Ux1GqSXB3MCYQKrxsmop-VAPbs8CdPM9e55yffbxCHHtWxeg2Iz0nYBsHJwKb2fQIj4Buvsj9qV5jchA4Qrlmq3D7SO4xzR6vT1_bWaNN2RoAbzk5x15DdMxFDMDzKe3_24PVx1FCkeFUCYtgL_FaLGKutuS3WX6V68xh3H8CgKtEKYEldx0W7BVONsGYiNPgOCSliI1v4XDEWx2IqQGQoKPwM8gQGLKDyvjZRHxWMmMXIGRkX8XU4tpdNpAOtGCu180iYMBiEo40m9RJ7zhlBC7OGhxfIFSDxroatg9_OJ8IKohd-FPsKexlUHv5-SVGqueoGHjg1lhvCcu0tENEYGPl2n_4B5igwpivS0zNEQDUc0LS6mmnN93VTFGxbLRuPOZW2rhAiWc6Nl50LZ2rNJZUUuUtakxxdYOALXsW3sSHF0g2oSxuHD01nbB2VvORnQ93_U-FBpfReMAUEJ4bRQ3egr2PFsaoEtLqw/4bc/_aAK8RJgTj6bVomq7yW3_g/h1/h001.wBg3xMff1TLdVuqxyC9zlYW2TnSuF-OS_2n-qzfOs9s)
2️⃣ **_Wisconsin:_ Democrats made key gains in Wisconsin Assembly and Senate **
Republicans lost their Senate supermajority & lost significant ground in the Assembly, despite marking Wisconsin as a top priority. These victories are crucial milestones in the DLCC’s long-term strategy to shift the balance of power in Wisconsin and [lay the groundwork for future gains to put majorities in play in 2026 → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hWwpbRT_BYJsLvsW0RbBHsbDbHDDWJoChQP3icQphBvdoPRFKIfQbAOdtlAXdJPovpcTmJ6I0NIrWxtoZAUbbkAtROBJBV3ca78IXp9E5x_K8c5jCgfObQR5my9RwzkE5vFz3TNe0Lw_d_Ccjufc9ezgJ0D022S9fKbU2xhQB-9GzkN3FrBZdjz3MGBGQDtdv4C_XDxKACJIE1_0fncizqXwN8wiJgLOWDLP-BGZnGme8QOMf5kbcNGElMrHlOkclo2n8-xaRcx5yf6QsclqBMm4NOotxO503w_6muUWwqkKVSDByR0vRmC86PjCxGVPcP_UyMDwGbg8VBDLmApr5Xtxf6leMq_26BznSaA8MXvSgsQcDgPTBpd-uQAmG-jMDHJerIskMgaJlZZTj2mmrnGq7T2Z7jh-ZSH181nfGx0jlSMi-3MIj0pjJVwCsl8qtyBCeE_L6DXP5Qsce83nDenfAt3vNigHEWocIvlOYcfNhdbvOwlJ28AnQrK5nyCyzO4r2jNv52Iy7FgPILAk6VEkm17WlcPQKeikKpKlqX65juTKKTaPTjc7i3d0nL8S3Kw/4bc/_aAK8RJgTj6bVomq7yW3_g/h2/h001.Yo-ZRuzsrdW45wzPq14HdupGtRLnvXF1KAbes_Yd390)
3️⃣ **_North Carolina:_ Democrats broke the North Carolina House supermajority **
Democrats broke the Republican supermajority in the NC House. This victory restores the veto power for incoming Democratic Governor Josh Stein and turns the tide after years of egregious veto overrides carried out by Republicans set on undermining fundamental freedoms and preserving their own power. [**Read more here →**](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW62rQa9Pz0Y7yher5LoycxlBqN-wPTxMoSMD51N_njNOBe5VasdR9ZHRzMsMs8UgO7oBzJF_kad2AvaUORJb5qggT4rIqKJCJyQp2LjeWTEmQ4Pa3rUvZCbTEK_n9QmveI-WV2Om79-XQLQYzuKv6uatcFTtf_si79Vninz0IBFUv7KPSYU5QHhiO8T9ULg9l1paxkSIauxK9gYZqxaKgt-M6JS9D05vDhWvE30nmtTwzlHwTA9q5g_5ScnSQYsKOvOt-QxBx3eiu77iw-k9ainUZGgmQ3Z0nu0MqoQVwt6KCaeT601qegfziTyY3ref-Up3TDGBXJnhrdskDzN4ISV5xuZHZSCUM22cHMxQY8J_EcySkgqaU-iaNZWBmZAzaqOx7sFMXsUNxoDReRut7ucIjBO9etXGBChJq4wdpB4yeppe-kGoCGbfx6W7yjO5-Zox4n6hD9XL00mqa8oYzVWdtlxrq4pqknfsJVgE8gJr4NhW2QOARjITn3G7HavPdeWTC2jVI0PvLwhDR7pDCjpFZEZhzsMeqIYt7I1ScMkcFT24zbKWlMlnq4YCOpTjyg/4bc/_aAK8RJgTj6bVomq7yW3_g/h3/h001.E5pIQY72UCR1B0iMovS_3MDS9xflwZLSpZ-S1ypH-W8)
4️⃣ **_Minnesota:_ Democrats defend Minnesota Senate Majority **
In a majority-making special election, DLCC Spotlight candidate Ann Johnson Stewart emerged victorious, protecting Democrats’ majority in the Minnesota Senate. [**To learn more click here →**](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW62rQa9Pz0Y7yher5LoycxnbL5dndfalsAVRkTHdyI2Dx4gc6jwev7lTkrfVowRBuvN-4qgNKerxJllQxn8xlmQp6m-hSyiktp7jjmexpbxkX6zi6wrtcPC0GWjaqtTXg0NvyQ0txG6VReINGVOdOdxFhHYOXyfITVFIx2zRsCttkwpQfba-xdEUBJw9yR7IlK6UdeQKQkgfXOTtAPuexHgTQu9P23WxIEI9BSo7JbAhxZhDIWEhCG7ehvWAu2kSFBTuUEsv2n_zmHPenm-wviFkwTuhv7qW2nsF7mJ6UQqlTa1BU4HVqIotZllSYaXX4_bbD0XA_CMVkNNkf2jffOA3KentTrmwq__79cJa1F_CJiBXma7kWQ4JNSRpywazC4Sgp9Z_uZU4tL8lCPjBLG5NrG5hi5wJEOHCClins_z4PfaM9UEe-A91_VAoNW97mGngpk7oSDsvu9agIjJKBdUIwdyWol6-7AnPKJ4EFd3S5_t5xC1HfglfGAgh-0eIZaFEtq3ZLSIYpnZLfUcBpG2_F6VQFJT6yvk_DhjsLL8d5kE0joFnLcolmc2HwuStEg/4bc/_aAK8RJgTj6bVomq7yW3_g/h4/h001.ZhfBV9zV3iIXm10u2-r1DO0kyAzJ7UJJ7f8poy03VQY)
🏳️⚧️ **_Breaking Barriers:_ Sarah McBride becomes the first Transgender Member of Congress **
DLCC Board Member Sarah McBride becomes the first openly transgender person to be elected federally – we could not be more proud of her and all she stands for. [Read more about her historic win here → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMRXuNSlhDxcvv3N1sDz0loluKN7sdZN-7orPDNziziQO7HwmzdlBTZGdab7Owcy77elK1A3KnHxJQHbLja7CX21ilVRbM827b6MRkr2SKsLnu67glB7Se-rCPlKGhIb5C8VhEbGVDP2Fj8i5W00m_Nk-Qp-Lq4-czEDirbQr7zYzHPSCniej61tkyD4xOcQ-pfURWFqGzhEWtDcF-QkQJbHIj0FHnXrRfq5UO7unGM1V8FXboaYmp19LOcSsqUCvMHL6bvoSPa5Oo7U3bGogvvwvwHTB3_3H_RmVNN52zA-mmnAiy_f9j5xoQ782fh6EWIwR04ep6t65kXXMlRXocHOOqY_vCA9ZHucbRLycSb_KlP-sZYEWNSdu4PYokevEpLOYrNrSyN1ZtupzzsNCVuyL9x6dWFTDwtjJiVNy0c4Jd4dC4WRHrMXyIzdmqtMhauKZVuzOoxc_7oyCxss_jJWcMsxtEXcJQyH_Y1vRpgqDoTvdtZxCMk3KZDT5Sgslv7eHt4O92OmjhZjaZBOGK-ZmxDEDs9UXYDF0hOZY0e9o_Y3-fH_AYeDbxLQOvkVAqc9xBIlL7f8qVOl8NA0OuOY/4bc/_aAK8RJgTj6bVomq7yW3_g/h5/h001.8k0HWPyjbVXrZVJqhn_HBN-sqYN5EObv6N70iS-0mNc)
🎉 **_Making History:_ New Mexico now has a majority women-led legislature **
Following Election Day, the chamber elected six new women, [making history with the most women legislators the state has seen → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMW6hc6B142yCjDWDxyQ5fJga3vI4NDEQhlDBtUlFIRyjxFhFuNI8Td2alCUeh_9-ayjkS4uyZFjyfDAJl3bZPzoBLke5k-0XGPUIeWn0sOzGok2SwINDggDONUg5AlBBMMbIYDqrA49k2NKvbzZyecbs6ynzc0jmWFl7vHUY_12iri4gBEgcLAxkw3qxVb_b10TL4O6je8t78kUc90ZC8BLTuAhc_rp_s3n7wKpKuSW-VQVEdOWJYSGXKP-zXpp-q2Vvv1cQwGhfgtZfb5RJ5aTR3Zl6fWMlqBvxx59XbLXynywBbMojELM44Z_BGpqAhDwMzMfK9QuTxU3jP9d5hhXMh3iUd2olctqp8b1WMj3hmfKNdVDPI6VkhvpkfyNzHqGKrOfEzKn9jNFFt98hrHmIhc73wLtyEWSroYMXN9ytpKj-C4egFXlITjrvCGk3CkTiFa2Ssj3uJ_JB3ZqZjYYXql1Gru1QE1wucc9gXjtnqK9j82Jr7-J5CtHsnzYidBz0KmXhZhDk2YMz-3kwfuEpyLMlWTsPVlB9-7efq7AsLmDVhYl5mcBQUR245h4ptTo0G16PIZP2OdhMXU11OxvT9_4g7H8Kyk3f-YLMClBBF5ZuNYIrkGFPlD40hL_03C7VOvzaRHyfeXQe2mXTbeY/4bc/_aAK8RJgTj6bVomq7yW3_g/h6/h001.JZNo3VseZmnPq5cOOzI_8yHYku07lzYOORdcj3Kjq94)
**🗳️ _Looking to upcoming races:_ Democrats work to build progressive power **
State Democrats are sure to face recounts, runoffs, and critical special elections in places like Virginia in the coming months. clean_body: Here's the latest edition of the DLCC's newsletter for Sunday, November 10th, 2024. | | | | | | 1️⃣ Pennsylvania: Democrats protect Pennsylvania House Majority In one of the most competitive state legislative chambers in the country, the DLCC and Pennsylvania House Democrats have successfully secured enough seats to protect their crucial majority and keep this chamber under Democratic control. 2️⃣ Wisconsin: Democrats made key gains in Wisconsin Assembly and Senate Republicans lost their Senate supermajority & lost significant ground in the Assembly, despite marking Wisconsin as a top priority. These victories are crucial milestones in the DLCC’s long-term strategy to shift the balance of power in Wisconsin and 3️⃣ North Carolina: Democrats broke the North Carolina House supermajority Democrats broke the Republican supermajority in the NC House. This victory restores the veto power for incoming Democratic Governor Josh Stein and turns the tide after years of egregious veto overrides carried out by Republicans set on undermining fundamental freedoms and preserving their own power. 4️⃣ Minnesota: Democrats defend Minnesota Senate Majority In a majority-making special election, DLCC Spotlight candidate Ann Johnson Stewart emerged victorious, protecting Democrats’ majority in the Minnesota Senate. 🏳️⚧️ Breaking Barriers: Sarah McBride becomes the first Transgender Member of Congress DLCC Board Member Sarah McBride becomes the first openly transgender person to be elected federally – we could not be more proud of her and all she stands for. 🎉 Making History: New Mexico now has a majority women-led legislature Following Election Day, the chamber elected six new women, 🗳️ Looking to upcoming races: Democrats work to build progressive power State Democrats are sure to face recounts, runoffs, and critical special elections in places like Virginia in the coming months. ****** | | ---|---|--- | Paid for by the Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee, dlcc.org. name: Kirsten Engel
subject: Today’s deadline
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
#

Hi Peter,
I’m still neck-and-neck with my opponent in the race for Arizona’s Sixth Congressional District.
_Everything is on the line._
Today is the last day we can cure lawful ballots — so our team is working around the clock.
**To make sure every lawful vote is counted fairly and securely, we need your help.**
[**Can you split a donation between our Recount Fund and Ruben Gallego to support our campaign as we work to determine a winner in this tossup race? Every bit of support makes a difference as we prepare for a potential recount.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-nov24-engel.l8HoIr&refcode=em241110-1-160e&t=1&akid=3556%2E734936%2ElzM4uf)

name: COURIER
subject: Peter, humbly asking you to read this message of resilience
body:
“Amid the cowardice of legacy media, let’s find brave journalists and support them.”

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886385/511526959/2130526953?refcode=emwrapper&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmIyNTczYjMtN2I5Zi1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aCu4GnWI0QPW668RzxxUE8gvzabG4FVynrIpjhN3j6o=&emci=643b90bf-f09d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=2b2573b3-7b9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=14016711)
Peter,
Anger. Sadness. Fear. Frustration.
**If Donald Trump’s victory this week left you feeling any of these emotions toward our politics or the legacy media that cover it, we’re right there with you.**
We know that many of you are feeling worried right now. We encourage everyone to take a moment to process what his victory means for our country and the values we hold dear.
In the meantime, we wanted to share COURIER’s Mark Jacob’s powerful Substack post, written the day after Election Day.
**Mark’s message is one of defiance and resilience, and we hope it offers you some inspiration for the fight ahead.**
[**If you have a moment, please read Mark’s message below. And if you’re able, please consider donating $25 or whatever you can to help us continue our mission in this critical time.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886386/511526960/-593472309?refcode=20241110-EM-MarkJacobResend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmIyNTczYjMtN2I5Zi1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aCu4GnWI0QPW668RzxxUE8gvzabG4FVynrIpjhN3j6o=)
In solidarity,
_The COURIER Team_
* * *
**_The fascists want us to shut up. Don’t._**
Amid the cowardice of legacy media, let’s find brave journalists and support them.
The Guardian’s headline read: _“Donald Trump defeats Kamala Harris to become the first convicted criminal to be elected U.S. president.”_
**We’ll have to see how long the Guardian keeps its press credentials after Trump takes power.**
We’ll also have to see if any other mainstream outlets will be as bold as the Guardian in telling the truth about Trump. The TV networks and newspapers know that they may face government harassment if they criticize him. Retribution is his brand, and they know it.
**The coming months and years will be a roll call for fearless journalists, and we don’t know how many will answer. But as we enter an era of deepening national madness, there will be essential stories to tell.**
Trump plans to appoint corrupt figures like Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to key positions and establish a Putin-style oligarchy. He vows to round up immigrants, put them in camps, and deport them—creating widespread suffering. His election endorses the second-class status of women, people of color, and trans people.
In Trump’s dictatorship, there will be many victims to interview. But will there be journalists willing to do so?
Or will we see a journalism of avoidance and distraction?
This election highlighted the power of mass media. But it was the power to misinform. Right-wing propaganda from Trump and Fox News convinced Americans that our economy was failing, that their kids were at risk of being “indoctrinated” in school, and that immigrants posed a violent threat.
**The truth was drowned out. The truth-tellers failed us.**
The responsible media neglected their duty to help the public understand facts. Instead of challenging the liars, they often treated lies as just another viewpoint. Major outlets normalized fascist misconduct and minimized the threat to our democracy. On Sunday shows, the same Republican liars were given platforms to deceive again and again.
**Legacy media allowed right-wing propagandists to create an alternative reality. They did nothing to reach those trapped in a bubble of disinformation, choosing profit over public interest._The Washington Post_ , _Los Angeles Times_ , and _USA Today_ took the cowardly route, avoiding endorsements that might anger Trump.**
**[Support Independent Journalism](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886387/511526961/-593472309?refcode=20241110-EM-MarkJacobResend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmIyNTczYjMtN2I5Zi1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aCu4GnWI0QPW668RzxxUE8gvzabG4FVynrIpjhN3j6o=)**
Now that Trump has his mandate, there’s little reason to believe those outlets will suddenly find their courage.
Look for Trump to welcome media moguls to Mar-a-Lago to bend the knee. He already has Elon Musk as a disinformation ally, and Mark Zuckerberg has retreated to the safe ground of downplaying political content on his platforms.
**_The New York Times_ faces a choice. It can oppose Trump and become a punching bag for his administration, or remain detached from the fight for democracy, burying internment camps under euphemisms like “involuntary gathering sites.”**
Our hope lies with the independent press, especially nonprofit outlets like _ProPublica_. In an era when legacy media have failed us, independent media have offered a lifeline. We need to support these outlets with our money and our passion. Read _The New Republic_. Read _Meidas Touch_. Read _COURIER_ —and more.
**We must keep posting on social media, speaking out about politics and injustice. We cannot ignore the disaster of these times. We must address it, or risk losing everything.**
Let’s stay connected through digital startups, newsletters, and independent platforms, beyond the interference of people like Elon Musk.
The fascists want us to accept the loss of our rights and the persecution of our fellow human beings.
Which is why we must keep speaking up.
And we won’t stop.
**[Donate ➡️](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886388/511526962/-593472309?refcode=20241110-EM-MarkJacobResend&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmIyNTczYjMtN2I5Zi1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aCu4GnWI0QPW668RzxxUE8gvzabG4FVynrIpjhN3j6o=)**
* * *
Email us: [**info@couriernewsroom.com**](mailto:info@couriernewsroom.com)
[**Become a member! **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886389/511526963/1028889122?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmIyNTczYjMtN2I5Zi1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aCu4GnWI0QPW668RzxxUE8gvzabG4FVynrIpjhN3j6o=&emci=643b90bf-f09d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=2b2573b3-7b9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=14016711)
This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

[**Receive Fewer Emails**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886390/511526964/1416945388?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmIyNTczYjMtN2I5Zi1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aCu4GnWI0QPW668RzxxUE8gvzabG4FVynrIpjhN3j6o=&emci=643b90bf-f09d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=2b2573b3-7b9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=14016711)
[**Update Your Information**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886391/511526965/1324474970?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmIyNTczYjMtN2I5Zi1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=aCu4GnWI0QPW668RzxxUE8gvzabG4FVynrIpjhN3j6o=&emci=643b90bf-f09d-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=2b2573b3-7b9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=14016711)

Look for Trump to welcome media moguls to Mar-a-Lago to bend the knee. He already has Elon Musk as a disinformation ally, and Mark Zuckerberg has retreated to the safe ground of downplaying political content on his platforms. The New York Times faces a choice. It can oppose Trump and become a punching bag for his administration, or remain detached from the fight for democracy, burying internment camps under euphemisms like “involuntary gathering sites.” Our hope lies with the independent press, especially nonprofit outlets like ProPublica. In an era when legacy media have failed us, independent media have offered a lifeline. We need to support these outlets with our money and our passion. Read The New Republic. Read Meidas Touch. Read COURIER —and more. We must keep posting on social media, speaking out about politics and injustice. We cannot ignore the disaster of these times. We must address it, or risk losing everything. Let’s stay connected through digital startups, newsletters, and independent platforms, beyond the interference of people like Elon Musk. The fascists want us to accept the loss of our rights and the persecution of our fellow human beings. Which is why we must keep speaking up. And we won’t stop. = Email us: mailto:info@couriernewsroom.com

name: Wiley Nickel
subject: This is a long note about the 2024 election, but I think it’s important to talk about. Please take a few minutes to read and respond:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina**
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886648/511527705/-488448398?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_GEN_AC_20241110_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTM0ZjYyMy03YzlmLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Emg7PfkYgNmLuUs7203tk3elNWhYm74cPNqyAKsL1mc=)
---
Peter — Like so many of you, I’ve spent the past few days trying to make sense of everything that happened on Tuesday. _What went wrong?
What could we have done differently?
How do we come back from this?_ I won’t pretend I have all the answers, but I think it’s important that we start having these conversations right away. And we need to have them out in the open so supporters like you, the lifeblood of our party, can be part of them. So here’s what I’m thinking five days later:
* * *
**I don’t think voters crave Trump; they just really hate inflation.** All year and all across the globe, the people and parties in power after COVID-19 have lost significant ground in a backlash against rising prices. From France to Japan to the UK, every incumbent party in a wealthy democracy faced major losses this year: _Chart courtesy of[Financial Times (FT)](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886649/511527706/-768831014?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_GEN_AC_20241110_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTM0ZjYyMy03YzlmLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Emg7PfkYgNmLuUs7203tk3elNWhYm74cPNqyAKsL1mc=&emci=76d53b12-be9e-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=1534f623-7c9f-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=28008880)_ I had hoped that Democrats would avoid the same outcome because _the Biden-Harris Administration has done a historically outstanding job at combating rising prices._ America’s inflation rate has dropped further and faster than nearly every other developed nation, and we’ve created millions of jobs while growing GDP in the meantime. **We have the strongest economy in the world and are years ahead of other countries when it comes to fighting inflation, but it wasn’t enough. Our challenge, as Democrats, is figuring out why.** _Why aren’t many voters feeling the benefits of falling prices and rising pay? What could we have done to better inform them about our economic wins and future plans? How was Trump able to earn their trust on the economy when our plan is so clearly better?_ Those are hard questions to answer, but when we figure them out, we will win.
* * *
**Democrats can run strong campaigns.** If you look at the chart I shared above, you can see that Democrats actually outperformed almost every incumbent party worldwide this year. I believe that’s because we know how to run strong and exciting campaigns. The crowds and the energy on the ground were electric. The organizing was phenomenal. In polling, our message helped shrink Trump’s lead or grow Democratic advantages on every major issue. Democrats still have many of the ingredients to win campaigns and grow our movement, but we need to find a way to overcome voters' economic distrust and break through in a new media environment.
* * *
**Trump’s win aside, North Carolina keeps getting bluer.** From 2020-2024, Trump’s share of the vote is likely to grow by 5-6% nationwide. In some states, like New York and Arizona, it appears to have grown even more. In North Carolina, Trump's vote share only grew 2%. **North Carolina Democrats outperformed the nation, and in a different political environment, I believe we would’ve flipped North Carolina’s electoral votes.** Don’t forget: North Carolina Democrats won at least five statewide races this year as we elected Josh Stein for Governor, Rachel Hunt for Lt. Governor, Jeff Jackson for Attorney General, Elaine Marshall for Secretary of State, and Mo Green for Superintendent. As of Friday, the statewide Supreme Court race remained too close to call. We overcame one of America’s most extreme legislative gerrymanders to gain ground in the state legislature and break a Republican supermajority. And we won North Carolina’s only competitive Congressional district, with my friend Don Davis decisively holding his U.S. House seat. Donald Trump’s win will earn the most press, but North Carolina Democrats have a lot to be proud of. That brings me to my final point...
* * *
**We’re going to win in 2026.** **In 2026, North Carolinians have the opportunity to help take back the United States Senate by unseating Thom Tillis.** I believe we’re well on our way to doing so. In 2024, we proved that the NC Democratic coalition is growing and organizing. We knocked hundreds-of-thousands of doors, made millions of calls, raised massive amounts of money, and held some of the most exciting rallies I’ve ever been part of. In 2026, the political environment will look very different. I don’t think it will take long for tuned-out voters to realize that Donald Trump’s economic plan is a failure — and that giving him a rubber-stamp senate is flat out dangerous. So if you’re looking for a way to take action right now, chipping in to support our eventual Senate nominee (and other NC Democrats on the ballot in 2026) is a great place to start: [**Please click here if you’d like to get back in the fight by preparing to win the Senate and turn North Carolina bright blue in 2026. Your donations fund early organizing, advertising, and outreach we need to keep NC trending blue.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886650/511527707/-1960556440?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_GEN_AC_20241110_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTM0ZjYyMy03YzlmLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Emg7PfkYgNmLuUs7203tk3elNWhYm74cPNqyAKsL1mc=) _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue, your donation to Wiley Nickel will process immediately:_ **** | | **[DONATE $10 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96886651/511527708/-1960556440?amount=10&express_lane=true&refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_GEN_AC_20241110_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTM0ZjYyMy03YzlmLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Emg7PfkYgNmLuUs7203tk3elNWhYm74cPNqyAKsL1mc=)**

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: We’ve been through a lot. But we’re not done yet.
body:
Peter: it’s tough.
I’m not going to lie to you. Our lead in Arizona continues to shrink, and there are still a considerable number of uncounted ballots across my state.
I don’t want to rile you up unnecessarily after everything you’ve been through over the last week, but I need all hands on deck now to ensure we hold the line.
I need you to chip in to my campaign as we fight to the final result. We have to secure this seat. Split $25 with The Real Texas PAC to help us get there. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-ruben?refcode=111024_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to The Real Texas PAC and Ruben Gallego will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-ruben?express_lane=true&refcode=111024_1&amount=5]

I don’t want to rile you up unnecessarily after everything you’ve been through over the last week, but I need all hands on deck now to ensure we hold the line. I need you to chip in to my campaign as we fight to the final result. We have to secure this seat. Split $25 with The Real Texas PAC to help us get there. [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to The Real Texas PAC and Ruben Gallego will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [] DONATE $10 [] DONATE $50 [] DONATE $100 [] DONATE $250 [] Other Amount [] Thank you, Ruben [] The Real Texas PAC 1150 N Loop 1604 W Ste 108-230 San Antonio, TX 78248 United States Contributions or gifts to The Real Texas PAC are not tax deductible. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, treasury funds, and federal government contractors are prohibited. Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

Hi Friend,
Don’t be mad. Be determined. And let’s get to work!
I know it’s been a difficult week. But the stakes for this nation, for our wives and daughters. For our husbands and sons. For our neighbors and friends. We can’t afford to walk away.
Donald Trump is looking to pack the Supreme Court with more hand-picked radical MAGA justices who will continue chipping away at our rights and freedoms for decades to come.
They already robbed women of the right to make their own healthcare decisions. What’s next contraception? Marriage equality?
This is Alan Cohn from Term Limit the Court, the national campaign working with 75 members of Congress, including Senator-Elect Adam Schiff and Hakeem Jeffries, to end lifetime appointments to SCOTUS and enact 18-year term limits.
We created a movement! Over 70% of Americans, Democrats, Republicans, and NPA’s say they want to see it happen.
We’re already back at work staging a major public pressure campaign to tap into public opinion and force Congress to act.
We’re already back at work raising the resources to flip the House and Senate and support candidates pledging to support the TERM Act.
Please join our fight to retire Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito!

name: Team GIFFORDS
subject: Kamala Harris said it best:
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
“This is not a time to throw up our hands. This is a time to roll up our sleeves.”
#

Kamala Harris said it best during her concession speech:
**_“This is not a time to throw up our hands. This is a time to roll up our sleeves.”_**
Another Donald Trump presidency is not going to be easy for our movement. But remember: We have faced this fight before.
We’ve confronted some tough challenges in our movement for gun violence prevention, and we know that with your help, we can take on new challenges together.
Tuesday night’s election results were obviously not what we wanted. We’re all tired after election season. But we must keep moving forward. Now is the time for us to prepare for the hard work ahead to defend our progress on gun safety.
[**Are you with us? If so, please sign our petition to show your commitment to defending the progress our gun violence prevention movement has made as we head into 2025 and beyond.**](https://go.giffords.org/signup/giffords_em_defendprogress_pet_nov24/?source=em241110-1-30&refcode2=9400.3935704.fu7A0c&akid=9400%2E3935704%2Efu7A0c)
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://go.giffords.org/signup/giffords_em_defendprogress_pet_nov24/?source=em241110-1-30&refcode2=9400.3935704.fu7A0c&akid=9400%2E3935704%2Efu7A0c)**
Thanks so much for standing with us,
The team at GIFFORDS PAC

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1&akid=9400%2E3935704%2Efu7A0c) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

name: The Democratic Party
subject: You deserve to know
body:
No question about it: the election did not go the way any of us had hoped.
---
Derek, No question about it: the election did not go the way any of us had hoped. We put everything we had into this election, knocked on millions of doors, and trained tens of thousands of volunteers — but ultimately came up short. This is a moment to learn, reflect, and rebuild. But it’s no time to sit back. As Vice President Harris said, we can’t throw up our hands, we have to roll up our sleeves. We cannot take a break. This work is more important than ever. Now, we have to pivot to an ask — an important one. **If you understand the importance of a strong Democratic Party — especially in this moment — then we are asking you to contribute to the Democratic National Committee today.** But before we share a link, you deserve to know that we will use your donation to strengthen our state and local Democratic parties that are tapped out after a tough election. We will use it to ensure we do not lose the organizing and tech infrastructure we’ve built up all across the country. We will use it to organize against destructive legislation and executive orders. And we will use it to put ourselves in a position to win local and statewide races next year, take back Congress in the next election, and the White House in 2028. We won't ask as often as we did before the election, but we do hope you can contribute in response to this request, especially before the new Congress and president are sworn in. [**Please reaffirm your commitment to a strong Democratic Party and standing up to Trump by making a $3 contribution to the Democratic National Committee today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2024?amount=3&refcode=em_20241110_2326_fr&refcode2=2328_22930333_3X840r&akid=2328%2E22930333%2E3X840r) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-november-2024?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20241110_2326_fr&refcode2=2328_22930333_3X840r&akid=2328%2E22930333%2E3X840r)**

name: The Collective PAC
subject: Peter, say congrats!

**Angela Alsobrooks just made history by becoming the FIRST Black woman to serve Maryland in the Senate!**

This victory has **DOUBLED** the number of Black women elected to the Senate **AND** will be the first time two Black women will serve at the same time.

Her dedication and resilience are an inspiration to us all, and we’re excited to see her leadership in action. We want to show how proud we are of this accomplishment by congratulating her!

**[Peter, will you sign our card for Senator-elect Alsobrooks and help us reach our goal of 10,000 signatures for her historic victory?](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3_iXaEBE6RoEOjHEa7LfvrWvKq3BYZWkfq9iNTUI1Z1sluxRWa-k8v8MS9m_bAg8i5K-m-0hdcEInJQqIT-zH_SVCOspMrAVokZ6GW09-lR4LCfPUrG9L6bP__K_F2YbresNP9ldVcfhHp8D5V45aCUUfOV58f_82tu_SXNviuzi0JJMqOyFhEjAI5Q-Pvthnq9oECWlUOMTzj0s9ehKrXNjhdxiHL9LCR0A5RvccpwYEksWSRVZbJ43o440mDuoeQZreA4Q1lSstE4Rn-Cugvcwrx2UnKeAKua-uCopWUzAnVpW0XbBj8FF8Z9S3snFzc3m0UZMgAsio_6ci4yLxwMRCZekfQQUaR9lKhvx5O6A88ofxh3Wp-gF309o-D8KNym66SR3GB4z8dZIgFqnKJYtSf528nJrKlEudpIKsIyoQ/4bc/iBPvLgb8QHa6rbVm93B5pg/h3/h001.xv9DZ_x_FeM3V49cyUtCCnkEq_8CUZjWa9Rwj9yHpOU)**

[](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtMtM9o7MpfQTM1SqECAoa9PnuvrCzoBy5DfIGWsplANbnjpBAv4hJoqiZg5D3GXPqF0KUxME24VIa7cTo_KRl5bsR05X_lylZfANDTOu4btHTrGgfB6GXB-FoRUDwcJMYaxgNuUuinQI_LdZtt7TyV0oqo_V-jdAZEA45-CSm16xAFYBCkwnptLjejDk3KB2Qtbze77RZpnSMpQUrmCKhaYfndAu5OzL0oMBs-gU_eQZlUgHC_UeFrLHWJkwXf4wl4sHnWRnj0HE4C5sLGADA7tAQCbHs3pVtAONdD6tALvdHCdcIiZY-AU8oisLWIzlbmFEngzqEsJHFJgttkzsgzBAW_jaYQVvtJArPkYV0f7DYPkaBr7QBC_9iNSrCnJkU/4bc/iBPvLgb8QHa6rbVm93B5pg/h4/h001.07a7BZ8EV4yrVsgNzlBe3jwYk-XBGpz9PCXlt22Blc0) The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 500 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. We strive to support the next wave of progressive and unapologetic Black candidates who will usher in a sea of change in our political system while helping America fulfill its promise of democracy by creating a government that will truly be for the people and of the people.

body:
| | | **BREAKING:** **Donald Trump wins.**
**We need to stick together:****[Pledge to OPPOSE Donald Trump and his MAGA allies>>](https://go.dscc.org/go/20522?ask=5&t=4&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20241110-AddYourName-TrumpWinsPledgeOppose-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20241110-AddYourName-TrumpWinsPledgeOppose-Pros-sl1&akid=18884%2E9716520%2EEI-jfO)**
| | [Add My Name](https://go.dscc.org/go/20522?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20241110-AddYourName-TrumpWinsPledgeOppose-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20241110-AddYourName-TrumpWinsPledgeOppose-Pros-sl1&akid=18884%2E9716520%2EEI-jfO)

Team, we can’t quit now. With Trump set to take back the White House, every Democrat needs to band together and vow to oppose him and his backward agenda.

**Now more than ever, it's time to make our voices heard, and we need your help:**

**[Pledge to OPPOSE Donald Trump and prove that Democrats will NEVER stop standing up for our democracy. Too much is at stake to quit now>>](https://go.dscc.org/go/20522?ask=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20241110-AddYourName-TrumpWinsPledgeOppose-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20241110-AddYourName-TrumpWinsPledgeOppose-Pros-sl1&akid=18884%2E9716520%2EEI-jfO)**

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: We’ve been through a lot. But we’re not done yet.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lucas Kunce for Missouri
body:
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/84114?refcode=em20241110-8661&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8661%2E614464%2EVCZHw5)
Peter — it’s tough.
I’m not going to lie to you. Our lead in Arizona continues to shrink, and there are still a considerable number of uncounted ballots across my state.
I don’t want to rile you up unnecessarily after everything you’ve been through over the last week, but I need all hands on deck now to ensure we hold the line.
**[I need you to chip in to my campaign as we fight to the final result. We have to secure this seat. * * *
# [Project 2025 Is Officially Here -- How to Fight Back](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=151453833&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNDUzODMzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzEyNTgzMjAsImV4cCI6MTczMzg1MDMyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.cqVuYOcrhDimDoM61aREK231weeXj4PLEkROjuJU-FQ)
### A recap of Project 2025 and how to counter its fascist policies
| | [Qasim Rashid](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Nov 10
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=151453833&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNDUzODMzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczMTI1ODMyMCwiZXhwIjoxNzMzODUwMzIwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.OW2WxVI4gfh2sJOxKH_EdJG0IWmfAwZDmUpG3HnIl0U&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=151453833&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNDUzODMzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzEyNTgzMjAsImV4cCI6MTczMzg1MDMyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.cqVuYOcrhDimDoM61aREK231weeXj4PLEkROjuJU-FQ&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=151453833&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNDUzODMzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzEyNTgzMjAsImV4cCI6MTczMzg1MDMyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.cqVuYOcrhDimDoM61aREK231weeXj4PLEkROjuJU-FQ)
---
| | [](https://substack..3DgLPJa4-gzEyWTwQeS2DExvTIuH_JbVvI4bU5LaOlg?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/project-2025-is-officially-here-how?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---
This article's headline is not clickbait, it is the sad reality, and we need to be ready. I read the 900+ page Project 2025 fascist manifesto so you don't have to. And over the coming weeks I will provide resources to navigate that fascist plan, with action items on how to fight back. Indeed, MAGA Republicans have made no secret of their intent to enforce Project 2025, and we must remain informed. **Let 's Address This.**
I am doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I welcome your support. **Subscribe below & join the movement.**
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..R6bC7nnWkqLA06r7nVQJ6gIlI1GlQ_gNyHROY-DfT2A?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=151453833)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d2c204ee-c78a-4fd2-80f2-64392ecb8ca8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A supporter holds a Project 2025 fan in the group's tent at the Iowa State Fair on Aug. 14, 2023. (Photo cred: Charlie Neibergall / Associated Press)
I have already published these detailed pieces on Project 2025. In fact, most of these were published before Biden stepped down. Below, I share the link to the full article, as well as an excerpt I wrote from each of those articles to summarize its main points.
* [Project 2025 Invites Fascism to America](https://substack.com/redirect/2feb63b7-feda-4bb5-b6fd-6c96cad47863?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

> With Project 2025, we are beyond mere arrival of fascism in America--we are on the cusp of full blown fascism. Project 2025 seeks to fully transform the United States into a banana republic and theocracy--both concepts repugnant to a U.S. Constitution committed to secular governance and equal access under the law. And given the success these right wing extremists have had in taking over the United States Supreme Court and hundreds of members of the United States Congress, it would be unwise to dismiss them as unserious in their attempts to win back the White House.
* [Project 2025 Attacks Abortion](https://substack.com/redirect/fc8f6992-9159-4f09-8fdb-adfbf70765b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

> Project 2025 disregards life, common sense, and science, and views essentially all abortion and reproductive access as evil. Based on what it defines as "_biblical values_ ," Project 2025 ultimately seeks to ban abortion altogether through defunding access, criminalizing access, and denying young people basic education and knowledge of how their own bodies work. It is dead set on transforming our secular government into a theocratic Christian nationalist fascist state. One in which women do not have the fundamental human right of personal autonomy, nor do physicians have the common sense ability to practice medical science according to actual science, not evangelical theocracy.
* [Project 2025 Attacks Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion](https://substack.com/redirect/3deed7c8-e034-441d-8d05-efd7a3569fa8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

> Project 2025 views DEI as the source of all ills in America. It is dead set on returning our government and business hiring practices to the Jim Crow era, a time when Black people, Asians, Latinos, and women had no meaningful opportunity for an equal shot at admission, a job, a loan, or a promotion. A Project 2025 administration will absolutely further normalize discrimination against people of color and women, not to mention LGBTQ+ Americans. And Project 2025 absolutely will fine, punish, and arguably imprison for alleged racial discrimination anyone who even mentions the concepts of DEI. It is imperative, therefore, that we do not allow this fascist manifesto to become American policy.
* [Project 2025 Attacks Public Education](https://substack.com/redirect/6c7e2f8b-560b-4075-9722-5f919bec9de8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

> Project 2025 fears a public education system that calls for meaningful racial justice and accountability, one that holds a mirror up to the injustices of white supremacy, and one that demands equal access to education for children of all races and socio-economic classes. It not only bans teaching about DEI, it demands prosecution of universities and employers who enact DEI practices. Throughout the manifesto, Project 2025 calls for a ban on any instruction that teaches about race or racism in the United States. In short, an education system under a Project 2025 administration would mean a gutting of public education, a transfer o wealth to predominantly white, predominately wealthy families for private school vouchers, an advancement of Christian nationalist theocratic education, a ban on teachings of racial justice, a green light for even violent anti-LGBTQ discrimination, and an exacerbation of existing racial segregation and underfunding of majority Black and Latino institutions.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..R6bC7nnWkqLA06r7nVQJ6gIlI1GlQ_gNyHROY-DfT2A?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=151453833)
### What Should We Be Doing Right Now?
With the election behind us, we must activate, organize, and invest if we hope to enact meaningful change for justice in the next election. Let's break down what that looks like with specific action items.
**Activate:** Get involved in your local politics. Whether it is your local Democratic committee, your local school board, or your local city council, get involved. Show up to the next meeting, meet your local elected officials, and understand the local issues impacting your community. Google is your friend in finding a local calendar of events. Use it. All politics is local, and it is critical you get involved locally.
**Organize:** Next, bring friends with you. You are the best ambassador for justice in your friend group--lead by example. Grab even one friend and drag them with you to the next school board meeting, city council session, or town hall in your local community. We need to build organizational capacity, and means each one reaches one to get involved. (And when your friend comes to the second meeting, it is their opportunity to reach one more and bring them with).
**Invest:** Finally, invest your time and your money into the people and organizations doing the front line work. If you have time to volunteer, make phone calls, knock doors, and sign petitions (e.g. [like this one to expand healthcare access](https://substack.com/redirect/43429a5c-45da-4ea4-8526-014458020dc1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)), do so. If you have the financial resources to donate to candidates committed to justice, donate to organizations working to protect our democracy, or to support writers and human rights lawyers working to stop fascism in America (e.g. [like yours truly](https://substack.com/redirect/257c4075-b6ba-4d91-a401-32b1c27d8f3e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)), do so. We may not have the billions of dollars in dark money from corporate autocrats, but we have strength in numbers and every individual donation has a lasting impact.
These are three small but effective steps to begin to right our ship. By meaningfully activating, organizing, and investing, we give ourselves the real opportunity of enacting transformational change in our politics. It will not happen over night, but what seems impossible today will appear inevitable tomorrow. Stay tuned for more updates on Project 2025 and calls to action to fight back.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=151453833&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxNDUzODMzLCJpYXQiOjE3MzEyNTgzMjAsImV4cCI6MTczMzg1MDMyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.cqVuYOcrhDimDoM61aREK231weeXj4PLEkROjuJU-FQ)
_As for me, I will be doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I cannot do it alone. I invite you to support my work and advocacy by subscribing below. Thank you for your trust and partnership._
